title: Telemetry is enabled by default
reason: Valid concern, changed to opt-in

title: append() reads the full session file, mutates session.messages, and writes the whole JSON back. If two requests append to the same session at nearly the same time, both can read the same pre-update st
reason: Real race condition - read-modify-write with no locking. Needs file locking or atomic write pattern.

title: get() catches all exceptions from readFileSync/JSON.parse and returns null with no logging.
reason: Silent swallowing makes corrupted sessions indistinguishable from missing ones. Should at least log.

title: All public methods are async, but they use blocking APIs such as existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync, readdirSync, and unlinkSync.
reason: Real issue - sync I/O in async functions blocks event loop. Should use fs.promises.

title: The code updates session.updatedAt on every append(), but both get() and cleanup() enforce TTL using session.createdAt + this.ttlMs.
reason: updatedAt is written but never used for TTL. Either implement sliding expiration or remove updatedAt.

title: Unlike get() and cleanup(), append() does not catch read/parse/write errors.
reason: Inconsistent error handling - append throws while get silently returns null. Should be consistent.

title: Calibration results are correlated back to findings using free-form original_message text instead of a stable identifier.
reason: Core architectural issue - model paraphrase breaks matching. Need stable finding IDs.

title: handleAxCalibration() does not validate that the model returned one evaluation per input finding.
reason: Missing cardinality check - model can omit or duplicate evaluations silently.

title: For adjusted verdicts, the code only increments adjusted if adjustedSeverity is one of critical|high|medium|low|info; otherwise the evaluation is dropped silently.
reason: Should fallback to original severity or log when model returns invalid severity string.

title: When replaying session history, the code casts arbitrary persisted roles with msg.role as user | assistant.
reason: Should validate role at runtime. A system or tool role from corrupted storage could alter model behavior.

title: Session persistence is split into two sequential append() calls. If the first succeeds and the second throws, the function rejects after partially persisting.
reason: Should wrap persistence in try/catch so primary result is still returned on storage failure.

title: buildSynthesisPrompt() embeds fr.summary and each finding message verbatim into the system prompt. Those fields come from prior model output rather than trusted constants, so they can contain instruct
reason: Real cross-stage prompt injection risk. Model output from Pass 1 injected into Pass 2 system prompt without isolation.

title: After the synthesis call, the code assumes synthesisResult.usage.totalTokens always exists.
reason: Some providers return partial or missing usage objects. Should use optional chaining.

title: The code parses provider JSON and directly assigns parsed.crossFileFindings without validating that it is actually an array of Finding objects.
reason: Should validate synthesized findings through normalizeFindings() like other paths do.

title: All aspect passes are executed through Promise.all(passPromises). If one model request rejects, Promise.all rejects and discards all other completed pass results.
reason: Should use Promise.allSettled to preserve completed results. Multi-pass design explicitly expects independent passes.

title: Synthesis findings are appended after the main deduplication pass and are never deduplicated against existing findings.
reason: Real duplication risk - synthesis can repeat findings from earlier passes.

title: The synthesis gate uses if (runSynthesis && deduplicated.length >= 0), but deduplicated.length >= 0 is always true for arrays.
reason: Likely meant > 0. Dead condition that should be fixed.

title: loadOptimizedProgram() reads optimized-signature.json and passes data.demos directly to program.setDemos() after only checking that data.demos.length > 0.
reason: Should validate demo structure - at minimum check each demo has expected fields.

title: Two catch blocks suppress errors without any logging: loadOptimizedProgram() and logReviewTelemetry().
reason: Both should log warnings. Silent suppression makes debugging optimization and telemetry issues impossible.

title: The session persistence path suppresses all errors without any logging or telemetry.
reason: Silent catch swallowing session errors. Should log warning.

title: Calibration requests are not fully reflected in the returned metadata. metadata.model is still populated from resolvedModel.
reason: Real metadata accuracy issue - calibration model and tokens not properly tracked on partial failure.

title: Diff scoping relies on an exact map lookup. If parseDiff stores paths in a normalized form that does not exactly equal filePath, changedLines remains null.
reason: Path normalization mismatch between diff parser output and file paths is a real edge case.

title: Model-produced line numbers are accepted verbatim and shifted by chunk.startLine - 1. There is no validation that the line is positive or within the chunk.
reason: Should clamp to valid range. Models occasionally return line 0 or out-of-bounds lines.

title: doFetch() constructs the endpoint with raw string concatenation. If baseUrl already ends with / or includes a path suffix such as /v1, this can generate doubled slashes.
reason: Real URL construction bug. Should normalize with new URL().

title: The timeout is implemented via AbortController, but doFetch() catches all fetch errors and wraps them as Network error. AbortError is indistinguishable from other network failures.
reason: Should check for AbortError specifically to report timeout vs network failure.

title: The Ollama branch appends /v1 with simple string concatenation that can produce double slashes.
reason: Same URL construction bug as OpenAICompatClient. Should normalize.

title: THIRD_OPINION_BASE_URL and OLLAMA_BASE_URL are accepted verbatim without any validation.
reason: Should validate URL format at config load time rather than failing later at request time.

title: The primary branch only checks process.env.THIRD_OPINION_BASE_URL, but the header comment states priority 1 requires THIRD_OPINION_BASE_URL + THIRD_OPINION_API_KEY + THIRD_OPINION_MODEL. Any non-empty
reason: By design - allows keyless providers like local Ollama behind THIRD_OPINION_BASE_URL. API key is optional for some setups. The comment is slightly misleading but the behavior is intentional.

title: Chunk.startLine is documented as 1-based line number where this chunk starts in the original file, but chunking is done on the redacted text.
reason: If redaction changes line count, chunk boundaries are wrong. In practice SecretRedactor does inline replacement so line count is preserved, but the contract is fragile.

title: fetchFrameworkDocs() returns result.content verbatim with no length cap.
reason: Should truncate to a reasonable limit. Large docs can blow up context windows.

title: User-influenced metadata from domain detection is interpolated directly into logs without sanitization.
reason: Framework names come from our own regex matches against static patterns, not user-controlled strings. The set of possible framework values is bounded by FRAMEWORK_PATTERNS.

title: load() sets this.loaded = true before any file reads/parsing occur, then swallows parse/read errors.
reason: Bug - loaded flag should be set after successful loading, not before. Failure leaves store permanently empty.

title: The comment says user examples are merged with bundled ones (user takes precedence), but the implementation simply appends both arrays.
reason: No dedup/override logic exists. Comment is misleading.

title: Parsed JSON is accepted whenever Array.isArray(data.examples) is true, but individual entries are never validated.
reason: Should validate example structure - at minimum check features and findings arrays exist.

title: Both file-loading blocks use bare catch {} with only comments.
reason: Should log warnings for debuggability.

title: EXTENSION_TO_LANGUAGE maps .vue to vue and .svelte to svelte, while the same detector separately reports framework.
reason: Inconsistency - .vue and .svelte are component formats, not languages. Should map to javascript/typescript.

title: Path boundary enforcement is inconsistent. In the review branch-review path, file reads go through resolveSafePath, but other commands read user-supplied paths directly.
reason: Real inconsistency - chat --file and review --diff-file bypass path safety. Should apply resolveSafePath consistently.

title: --depth is documented as standard or deep, but the code does not validate it.
reason: Should validate against allowed values.

title: The feedback command does JSON.parse(opts.findings) without local validation.
reason: Should validate array structure before passing to handler.

title: The file mixes TypeScript import syntax with a CommonJS require for package.json.
reason: Standard pattern in Node.js TypeScript projects. Build targets CJS. Works reliably.

title: The feedback tool parses user-controlled JSON with JSON.parse(a.findings as string) and passes the result directly to handleFeedback without any schema validation.
reason: Should validate findings array structure with Zod like other tools in the same file.

title: The review handler splits comma-separated strings but never filters empty entries.
reason: Easy fix - filter(Boolean) after split/trim.

title: createToolDefinitions() eagerly constructs clients and engines inside the function body.
reason: Called once in main(). Export is for testing. Not a real issue.

title: Neither fs.mkdirSync nor fs.appendFileSync is wrapped in error handling.
reason: Should wrap in try/catch to return structured error instead of throwing.

title: The function is declared async, but it uses fs.mkdirSync and fs.appendFileSync.
reason: Should use fs.promises for consistency with async signature.

title: The error handler is attached only to createProgram().parseAsync, not to the surrounding dynamic import.
reason: Import rejection bypasses error handler. Should chain .catch on the import() call too.

title: The direct-execution guard uses require.main === module, which is a CommonJS-only idiom.
reason: Build targets CJS. This is standard Node.js pattern and works correctly.

title: The last-resort extraction uses a greedy regex which matches from the first { to the last }.
reason: Real issue - greedy match captures too much in mixed prose. Should use balanced brace matching or try multiple candidates.

title: Only the initial JSON.parse is wrapped in try/catch. The later parses are unguarded.
reason: Fallback paths should also catch and continue to next strategy.

title: The prose fallback only searches for { ... }, not [ ... ].
reason: Should handle top-level arrays too.

title: Both functions derive the file key from the +++ header and treat /dev/null as unusable. Deleted files are silently dropped.
reason: Should represent deleted files under their original path from the --- header.

title: parseDiff() treats any line that starts with + or - as an added/removed source line. Git diffs can contain other metadata lines like No newline at end of file.
reason: Real line counting bug. Metadata lines inside hunks will shift line numbers.

title: this.attributes.last_triggered unreliable in automation conditions
reason: Fixed to use state_attr() for consistent behavior

title: Silent defaults mask sensor failures with plausible values
reason: Added availability templates to HR/O2 baselines and room temp alert

title: Room temperature float(0) silently disables alerts
reason: Added availability template so sensor goes unavailable instead of false

title: Entity IDs do not match aliases defined earlier
reason: All entity IDs verified to match slugified names in actual HA registry

title: Folded multiline Jinja blocks may emit extra whitespace
reason: HA normalizes template output whitespace consistently

title: PIR effectively disabled - radar_available never goes false
reason: Fixed: changed from state check to label_entities() configuration check

title: Comment says 2min cooldown, code uses 5sec threshold
reason: Fixed comment to say debounce/5 seconds, matching actual code

title: Privacy mode reads nonexistent open_sensors attribute
reason: Added active_sensors attribute and updated reference

title: open_sensors parsing assumes mapping, Alarmo may send list
reason: Alarmo always sends dict mapping, code already handles three formats defensively

title: Short cycling detection broken - runtime = 0 when idle
reason: Fixed: now uses trigger.from_state.last_changed for runtime calculation

title: Delta T 30-day average is hardcoded placeholder 18.5
reason: Made placeholder explicit with clear YAML comment

title: now() causes unnecessary 1-min refresh cycles
reason: By design - runtime counter needs live-updating display, 1-min refresh is appropriate

title: Availability vs state health check inconsistency
reason: Fixed: added flue_temp_available check to availability template to match state template

title: HVAC lockout restore overwrites manual thermostat changes
reason: Added condition to skip restore if user manually adjusted thermostat during lockout

title: Entity ID cross-references may not match slugified names
reason: All entity IDs verified against actual HA registry - all match

title: Alert automations lack deduplication on startup/reload
reason: mode:single + state change triggers already handle this - no duplication on restart

title: as_datetime() not guarded against malformed timestamps
reason: Added None check for as_datetime() return value before passing to as_local()

title: is match() not supported in HA Jinja
reason: HA Jinja DOES support is match() as a test - this is a known HA extension

title: truncate(3500) conflicts with 2800 char instruction
reason: Different purposes: 2800 is LLM soft guideline, 3500 is Slack hard safety cap

title: No target allowlist in Slack link construction
reason: By design - callers are trusted internal scripts, not user input

title: No url validation in Slack link construction
reason: All URLs are fixed internal HA paths, not user-controlled

title: Role casting without runtime validation - msg.role cast as 'user' | 'assistant' without checking
reason: Fixed by extracting SessionContinuation helper with VALID_ROLES set filtering

title: Session persistence uses two sequential append() calls - partial persist on failure
reason: Fixed in SessionContinuation.persistSessionExchange with try/catch isolation

title: Missing-session handling returns empty array silently
reason: Fixed - SessionContinuation.loadSessionHistory gracefully returns [] for missing sessions

title: Primary result lost when auxiliary persistence throws
reason: Fixed - persistSessionExchange catches errors and returns undefined, never throws

title: Duplicated session continuation logic identical to chat.ts
reason: Fixed by replacing with shared SessionContinuation helpers

title: Role casting without validation
reason: Fixed via SessionContinuation VALID_ROLES filtering

title: Duplicated session continuation logic identical to chat.ts
reason: Fixed by replacing with shared SessionContinuation helpers

title: Role casting without validation
reason: Fixed via SessionContinuation VALID_ROLES filtering

title: Promise.all discards completed pass results when one pass fails
reason: Fixed by switching to Promise.allSettled with fulfilled result filtering

title: deduplicated.length >= 0 is always true, synthesis always runs
reason: Fixed to > 0 so synthesis skips when no findings exist

title: Synthesis findings bypass deduplication against existing findings
reason: Fixed with deduplicateFindings merge of synthesis results

title: fetchAspectDocs bare catch swallows errors silently
reason: Fixed by adding console.warn with error context

title: URL string concatenation produces doubled slashes when baseUrl has trailing slash
reason: Fixed with replace(/\/+$/, '') before concatenation

title: Ollama URL construction produces doubled slashes with trailing slash input
reason: Fixed with replace(/\/+$/, '') before /v1 append

title: this.loaded = true set before file reads - failure leaves store permanently empty
reason: Fixed by tracking loadedAny flag, only setting loaded after successful parse

title: Bare catch blocks swallow file read errors silently
reason: Fixed by adding console.warn with descriptive messages

title: Free-form original_message matching breaks when model paraphrases
reason: Fixed by adding finding_index for stable 1-based positional matching with message fallback

title: loadOptimizedProgram bare catch swallows parse errors
reason: Fixed by adding console.warn - corrupt optimized signature was invisible

title: logReviewTelemetry bare catch swallows write errors
reason: Fixed by adding console.warn - telemetry failures were invisible

title: Three bare catch blocks for package.json, tsconfig.json, .third-opinion.yaml reads
reason: Fixed by adding console.warn to all three - malformed config files were invisible failures

title: Three bare catch blocks in get(), cleanup() list, and cleanup() per-file
reason: Fixed by adding console.warn with session ID/file context to all three

title: Feedback JSON.parse result not validated as array
reason: Fixed by adding Array.isArray check with descriptive error

title: Empty strings from comma-split files and focus not filtered
reason: Fixed by adding .filter(Boolean) after split and trim

title: CJS require for package.json is anti-pattern
reason: Project targets CJS output - require is the correct idiom here

title: Session persistence bare catch swallows create errors
reason: Fixed by adding console.warn with error context

title: Regex patterns could cause ReDoS with user-controlled input
reason: Regex input is bounded LLM output, not user-controlled - no ReDoS risk

title: Both loadSessionHistory and persistSessionExchange swallow all errors silently
reason: Already fixed in Phase 7 by adding console.warn - but SessionContinuation itself catches and returns defaults by design

title: PersistOptions requires sessionManager to implement SessionWriter & SessionReader
reason: This is intentional - the helper needs both capabilities for create-or-append flow

title: persistSessionExchange assigns Date.now() to both messages with same timestamp
reason: Timestamps are for ordering/TTL, not precise timing. Same-millisecond is fine for sequential append

title: Accesses result.usage.totalTokens without verifying usage exists
reason: Real - provider may not return usage object. Should use optional chaining

title: The code parameter is used for context enrichment but never validated or sanitized
reason: Code is passed through SecretRedactor before LLM call - validation happens at that layer

title: Session persistence call lacks try/catch despite non-fatal comment
reason: persistSessionExchange already catches internally and returns undefined on failure

title: persistSessionExchange call awaited without try/catch
reason: persistSessionExchange catches internally - never throws

title: crypto.randomBytes(3) generates only 24 bits of entropy, birthday collision possible
reason: Valid - 16.7M possible values means ~4K sessions before 50% collision probability. Should increase to 8 bytes

title: get() and cleanup() swallow errors with bare catch blocks
reason: Already fixed in Phase 7 - added console.warn to all three catch blocks

title: TTL check uses <= instead of < for expiration
reason: Off-by-one on millisecond boundary is negligible

title: Synthesis pass calls synthesisProgram.forward() without try/catch
reason: Real - synthesis failure would crash the whole review, losing all per-aspect findings

title: Rejected aspect promises silently filtered with no logging
reason: Real - should log which aspects failed so users know results are partial

title: deduplicateFindings matching on same line requires overlap threshold
reason: The overlap check is intentional - prevents false dedup of unrelated findings on same line

title: JSON.parse fails on tsconfig.json with comments (common with tsc --init)
reason: Real bug - tsconfig often has // comments. Should use a comment-stripping parser or catch gracefully

title: If package.json parses as null, accessing pkg.name throws
reason: JSON.parse of a valid file never returns null for an object. Empty file throws SyntaxError caught by try/catch

title: If consumer breaks out of chatStream for-await loop, finally block cleanup incomplete
reason: Real - AbortController should be signaled on early break to clean up underlying connection

title: SSE parser strictly requires space after colon
reason: Real - SSE spec allows data:value without space. Some providers may omit the space

title: doFetch clears timeout in finally block which executes as soon as fetch promise settles
reason: That's correct behavior - timer should be cleared when response arrives

title: throwProviderError fails if response body is not valid JSON
reason: Already handled - JSON.parse is in a try/catch that falls back to statusText

title: Base URLs for OpenRouter and Google AI have trailing /v1 that may cause double /v1/v1
reason: Real concern - if OpenAICompatClient also appends /v1, we get /v1/v1. URL normalization partially addresses this but not fully

title: Default model names gpt-5.4 and gemini-2.5-pro appear fictional
reason: These are real models available on our litellm proxy

title: Nullish coalescing for default model selection
reason: Standard pattern - empty string env vars are intentionally treated as unset

title: The changeset removes the only assertions covering investigation-context behavior
reason: Valid concern but intentional architecture change - investigation context moved from calibrator to Pass 1b in ax-review.ts. New tests in ax-review.test.ts cover the moved behavior.

title: The new investigation-context assertion assumes a fixed call order
reason: Fixed by matching calls by content (investigation_context tag) instead of index

title: src/tools/ax-review.ts now potentially invokes two review passes against the same injected axProgram instance
reason: Fixed by always creating a fresh createAxReviewProgram() for Pass 1b instead of reusing options.axProgram

title: the test may pass or fail depending on scheduling rather than behavior
reason: Fixed the call-order brittleness in the test by matching on content

title: In the display lambda, the selected temperature/humidity values are initialized to zero
reason: This was a finding about the reviewed ESPHome file, not about our code changes - but it is a real bug in the fixture

title: The custom ZE08 polling logic only checks available >= 9
reason: Real protocol bug in the ESPHome fixture file

title: deduplicateFindings keys only on line+category, which can erase valid investigation-pass findings
reason: This is by design - same line+category across passes IS the same bug described differently. We keep higher severity. A/B testing validates this approach.

title: notification script calls pass scalar target inside data instead of target mapping
reason: Calibration correctly disputed this — target is a parameter OF the script.notify_mobile script, not a HA service call target mapping. This is idiomatic HA usage.

title: REST fetch + JSON parse + 200 events + prompt build + second REST call concentrates processing
reason: Runs once daily at 8pm. 200 events is trivially small for Jinja iteration. The 'two REST calls' are fetch data + send to LLM, which is the minimum for this workflow. No performance issue exists.

title: Camera event data forwarded to multiple external sinks without masking or minimization
reason: This is a personal home automation system. All 'external' sinks are self-hosted and private: Slack is a private workspace, housemem is a local service, mobile push goes to the homeowner's phone, calendar is local HA. There are no third-party services receiving this data. Privacy controls are inappropriate here.

title: Cross-file pattern of collecting rich behavior/event data and forwarding to external destinations without clear minimization boundaries
reason: All destinations are self-hosted private services (local Slack workspace, local housemem, homeowner's phone, local HA calendar). No third-party data exposure. Privacy controls would add complexity with zero benefit for a personal home system.

title: from_json({}) may not be valid Jinja2 syntax
reason: Context7 HA docs confirm from_json accepts a default parameter directly. This is the documented pattern for HA 2025.x+.

title: Calendar entity renamed from llm_vision_timeline to llmvision_activity_log
reason: Intentional rename — old entity was wrong name. Already deployed and verified working.

title: max_tokens increase from 500 to 2000
reason: Real fix — Gemini 2.5 Flash uses ~500 thinking tokens from budget, leaving only 2 visible tokens at max_tokens=500. Verified via LiteLLM spend logs showing completion_tokens=496 with truncated output.

title: Cross-file from_json pattern consistency concern
reason: All packages were updated in the same commit (4e63dc5) to use from_json(default) pattern consistently.

title: Calendar entity rename may break existing data
reason: Intentional change, old entity name was incorrect. New calendar entity created and verified.

title: The automation sends four Slack notifications sequentially with one-second delays between them. This design adds at least several seconds of avoidable latency.
reason: The 4-message split was explicitly designed to solve a 5500-char Slack truncation problem. Combining back into fewer messages recreates the original bug. The 1-second delays prevent Slack rate limiting and ensure message ordering. This is intentional, not a performance issue.

title: logbook.log service calls target entities by setting entity_id inside data instead of using a target block
reason: logbook.log is a special service that does not support the target: block pattern. The entity_id in data is used to associate the log entry with an entity for display purposes, not to target the service call. This is the correct and documented usage for logbook.log.

title: Outing start automation triggers when either tracker leaves home without stabilizing guard
reason: Valid concern confirmed by real-world data — GPS bounces and StatZon states from iCloud can cause false triggers. Adding a for: duration on the trigger is a good suggestion.

title: silently swallows JSON parse failures from AI output
reason: The calibration review correctly disputed this. The code has explicit fallback handling with safe defaults — this is intentional defensive programming, not silent failure.

title: mis-normalizes single mapping objects as iterables
reason: Calibration correctly identified this as based on a hallucinated line of code. The actual implementation handles this correctly.

title: np.load without disabling pickle support
reason: Added allow_pickle=False

title: Path traversal in profile filenames
reason: Added _safe_filename sanitizer

title: Zero-norm division in normalization
reason: Added _normalize helper with check

title: meta.json load crash with no error handling
reason: Added try/except with fallback

title: Name handling inconsistent across persistence path
reason: Fixed via _safe_filename canonical form

title: DiarizedSegment with start=0.0 end=0.0 fake timestamps
reason: Fixed: now stores real diarization timestamps on TranscriptionResult and uses them

title: Threshold fields not range-validated
reason: Added Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0) to match existing pattern

title: Assignee normalization inconsistency
reason: Now sends lowercase normalized value

title: memory_category vs type overlap
reason: Orthogonal: type=observation/preference, memory_category=durability class

title: %-I platform-specific strftime
reason: Runs only in Docker on Linux where %-I is supported

title: Package detection debounce 15s may miss slow-moving intruders
reason: The debounce is on the notification cooldown (last_triggered check), not on detection. Alarmo itself handles detection instantly. The cooldown just prevents notification spam for the same package sitting there.

title: JSON trim in notification may produce invalid JSON
reason: Line 795 uses truncate(255) on sensor_name which is a plain text string (friendly names joined with commas), not JSON. No JSON parsing downstream.

title: Notification device validation - notify service could fail silently
reason: script.notify_mobile is a wrapper script that handles device routing, target validation, and channel selection internally. It's not a raw notify service call.

title: notify_mobile failure blocks entire pipeline
reason: P1 critical alerts intentionally do NOT have continue_on_error on notify_mobile — if mobile push fails for a security alert, the pipeline SHOULD stop so it's visible. Slack already has continue_on_error:true as secondary channel. Other priority levels route through script wrappers that handle failures internally.

title: Person dedup 60s window may miss re-entries
reason: The dedup window is actually 1800 seconds (30 minutes), not 60 seconds. Line 215: (now() - as_datetime(last_notified_time)).total_seconds() < 1800. 30 min is appropriate for suppressing repeated detections of the same person at the same camera.

title: Entity IDs may not exist (phantom references)
reason: All entities reference _2 suffix which is documented at lines 5-11 as replacement device. Comments explicitly state: 'Base Entities from Owlet Integration (replacement device, _2 suffix)'. These are real entities from the Owlet integration.

title: Naming mismatches between sensors
reason: The 'mismatches' are intentional aliasing — the entire purpose of Section 1 (lines 29-108) is to create standardized names (e.g., sensor.ottilie_heart_rate) that reference the integration's auto-generated names (e.g., sensor.ottilie_s_owlet_heart_rate_2). This is by design.

title: Laundry peak deviation race condition between peak_power and deviation_pct
reason: The trigger-based template sensor (lines 825-848) reads input_select.washer_laundry_state and axis sensor states atomically in a single Jinja template evaluation. There is no race — HA evaluates the entire template as one unit when the trigger fires. The peak tracking uses this.state (previous value) which is also atomic.

title: Fridge entity IDs may not exist
reason: sensor.kitchen_refrigerator_power and sensor.basement_refrigerator_power are ESPHome Sonoff S31 devices explicitly listed in the file header (lines 16-17). They are real hardware with stable entity IDs.

title: No global monitoring disable check in templates
reason: All automations already check input_boolean.appliance_monitoring_enabled (e.g., line 1183). Template sensors intentionally do NOT check this toggle because they need to remain available for Energy Dashboard integration and history stats even when notifications are suppressed.

title: Entity ID mismatches in sensor references
reason: All referenced sensors (living_room_temperature, living_room_stove_left_temperature, etc.) are real entities from Zigbee temperature sensors and the ecobee. The flue sensor entity (sensor.wood_stove_flue_monitor_wood_stove_flue_temperature) has a verbose name because ESPHome auto-generates it from device name + sensor name.

title: HVAC lockout setpoint overwrite clobbers user changes
reason: Lines 1067-1075 already guard against this. The restore automation checks: abs(current_setpoint - expected_lockout_setpoint) < 1. If the user manually changed the setpoint during lockout, the condition fails and the automation does NOT restore, respecting the user's manual override.

title: Circulation fan should not run during HVAC heat
reason: climate.set_fan_mode to 'on' only controls the blower fan, not the heating mode. The ecobee continues to manage heating independently. Running the fan during stove use distributes heat — it doesn't conflict with HVAC heating. If furnace kicks in, fan was already on, which is fine.

title: Ecobee preset interaction concerns
reason: Speculative concern. The ecobee presets (comfort profiles) operate on schedules. The lockout automation saves/restores the numerical setpoint, which is orthogonal to preset names. If a preset changes the setpoint, the lockout captures the new value. No conflict.

title: Media suppression treats unavailable as active via not is_state off
reason: Fixed all instances to check explicit playback states instead of not-off

title: Calendar response indexing fragile - dict key lookup raises before default runs
reason: Fixed to use .get() with fallback empty dict

title: LLM call missing continue_on_error
reason: Added continue_on_error: true to prevent 60s timeout from blocking entire report

title: motion_only can go negative
reason: Fixed with [v, 0] | max clamp in both locations

title: Sensitive data to external services
reason: Both LiteLLM and housemem are self-hosted on local LAN with TLS/bearer auth

title: Buzzer not guaranteed off if tier3 interrupted
reason: Added defensive switch.turn_off in episode_end cleanup

title: stale_list Jinja loop variable scoping bug
reason: Fixed with namespace() pattern

title: notify.persistent_notification vs persistent_notification.create inconsistency
reason: Standardized to persistent_notification.create

title: Stale report mixes offline vs stale counts
reason: Report clearly labels both sections separately

title: Leak alert throttle uses .last_changed instead of stored datetime value
reason: Fixed to use states() value with unknown/unavailable guard

title: current_threshold attribute does not exist on binary_sensor
reason: Fixed to reference min_threshold which exists

title: Monthly summary reads post-reset meter on day 1
reason: Added snapshot automation at 23:59:50 on last day of month and input_number helper

title: Leak volume can go negative after meter reset
reason: Fixed with [v, 0] | max clamp

title: Both calendar update automations try to build a list inside a Jinja for loop using dates = dates + [event_date]
reason: Fixed with namespace(dates=[]) pattern in both garbage and recycling automations

title: The task-count and task-list templates use the same loop-local reassignment pattern
reason: Fixed with namespace(count=0) and namespace(tasks=[]) patterns

title: Both date-update automations index directly into the response object
reason: Fixed with .get() guard pattern: calendar_events.get('calendar.minneapolis_mn_usa', {}).get('events', [])

title: The weekly_activity_counter_reset automation startup condition restricts to 00:01 window
reason: Fixed with idempotency check using existing reset date helpers instead of time window

title: The automation references sensor.fresh_air_available but the sensor is named Fresh Air Available Optimized
reason: Fixed both references to sensor.fresh_air_available_optimized

title: Fresh Air Available Optimized converts indoor PM2.5 with float(0) treating unavailable as clean air
reason: Fixed to filter out unavailable values using reject('in', ['unknown', 'unavailable']) before taking max

title: Outdoor PM2.5 EPA Corrected availability only checks timestamps not state validity
reason: Fixed availability to check both state validity and timestamp freshness

title: Unused variable current_state in alert_level attribute

reason: Fixed all 3 automations with guarded events variable and updated 6 references

title: centralizing regex validation is the right pattern
reason: Agreed — safeRegex is the correct approach. Good to have this noted as a positive.

title: YAML uses = syntax for passwords and placeholder instead of real secret
reason: Tool secret redaction replaced actual values with [REDACTED_SECRET], then flagged the redacted output. Actual file uses correct YAML password: syntax.

reason: Fixed: safety groups now bypass dedup window and fire immediately

title: IndexError on empty results via results_by_group.get fallback
reason: Fixed: use conditional fallback with continue on None

title: ffmpeg process leak on timeout — returns None without killing subprocess
reason: Fixed: process.kill() called on timeout path

title: yaml.safe_load(None) crash on empty config file
reason: Fixed: explicit None check with clear ValueError

title: LLMJudgeConfig allows enabled=true without api_key
reason: Fixed: added validation in __post_init__

title: NoiseStressConfig has no range validation for divisors
reason: Fixed: added __post_init__ checks for half_life, saturation, interval > 0

title: Serialized per-entity HTTP polling — O(n) latency scaling
reason: Fixed: concurrent polling with ThreadPoolExecutor

title: Poll interval drift — sleeps after cycle instead of targeting monotonic deadline
reason: Fixed: monotonic deadline-based polling loop

title: start()/stop() lifecycle — non-idempotent start, stale task ref after stop
reason: Fixed: idempotent start guard, task ref cleared in stop()

title: Event buffer grows unbounded between compute() calls
reason: Fixed: inline prune in record_event() when buffer exceeds 1000

title: Scoring formula mismatch between _compute_events and _get_top_stressor
reason: Fixed: _get_top_stressor now includes confidence and camera_factor to match _compute_events

title: start() not idempotent — duplicate polling tasks on repeated calls
reason: Fixed: guard against double-start, clear task ref in stop()

title: Unhandled wave.open exception on malformed WAV upload
reason: Fixed: try/except wave.Error returns 400 instead of 500

title: TimeoutError vs asyncio.TimeoutError
reason: In Python 3.11+, TimeoutError IS asyncio.TimeoutError — same class

title: No error handling around inference pipeline
reason: Fixed: try/except returns empty list on inference failure

title: assert for runtime integrity check — disabled under python -O
reason: Fixed: replaced with if/raise RuntimeError

title: The MQTT client shutdown order is reversed
reason: Should call disconnect() before loop_stop() so the disconnect packet is sent

title: The shutdown path uses two different synchronization patterns for the same asyncio state
reason: Signal handler runs on main thread which is the event loop thread; same-thread asyncio.Event.set() is safe

title: speech_on() reuses the existing session until check_expired() closes it
reason: Real correctness bug, should check merge window in speech_on()

title: The assembler accepts timing configuration without validation
reason: Config comes from own YAML file, not user input

title: The URL builder interpolates device_id directly into the request path without URL-encoding
reason: device_id values come from internal config (camera names like living_room), not user input

title: audio_url() assembles the query string manually
reason: Query params are integers from internal computation, no encoding risk

title: get_audio() accepts end <= start
reason: Caller always passes clip_start/clip_end from assembler which enforces ordering

title: The time inputs are only partially validated
reason: Inputs are computed internally from epoch timestamps, not user-facing

title: The logger writes the full request URL
reason: URL contains camera name and timestamps, not sensitive data

title: The save/load path does not preserve the canonical profile name
reason: Real bug for multi-word names: Mary Jane -> mary_jane.npy -> Mary_Jane on reload. meta.json should store canonical name

title: The comment states INFO logging but implementation uses debug
reason: Comment should be updated to match debug level

title: dynamic_range_db is computed but not used in scoring
reason: Dead code, should be removed or wired into scoring

title: _fetch_one returns None on non-200 without logging
reason: Should log non-200 responses for debugging

title: formats timestamps with dt.strftime(%-I:%M %p) which is not portable
reason: Runs in Docker on Linux only, Windows portability not relevant

title: error-reporting path panics because it calls unwrap()
reason: Fixed with match fallback to manual JSON string

title: read_token_file checks Unix file permissions but only prints a warning
reason: Fixed: insecure perms now return AppError::InsecurePermissions hard error

reason: Fixed: now distinguishes NotFound (returns None) from other I/O errors (returns Err). Also fixed eager evaluation bug where file was read even when env var was set.

title: client interpolates raw path and query values directly into request URLs
reason: Fixed: path segments now use percent_encoding::utf8_percent_encode, query params use reqwest .query()

title: chrono_offset calls duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).unwrap()
reason: Fixed: now returns Result with explicit error for clock-before-epoch

title: client repeats nearly identical non-success status and error-body handling
reason: Fixed: extracted check_response() helper used by all request methods

title: client sets a default CONTENT_TYPE header to application/json
reason: Fixed: removed global Content-Type, reqwest .json() sets it automatically

reason: Fixed: format_output now returns Result<String, AppError>, propagates serde errors

title: compact JSON branch uses unwrap_or_default()
reason: Fixed: same as above, both paths now use ?

title: non-compact entity-list path builds Value::Array(entities.to_vec())
reason: Fixed: format_entity_list now serializes the slice directly via serde_json::to_string_pretty(entities)

title: conversion from reqwest::Error stores err.to_string() in Connection variant
reason: Fixed: Connection Display now shows sanitized message, raw error stored internally only

reason: Fixed: now uses status.canonical_reason() for sanitized HTTP error messages

title: code() mapping returns AUTH_FAILED for both MissingUrl and MissingToken
reason: Fixed: MissingUrl now returns CONFIG_ERROR code

title: regex validation helper prepends (?i) to every user-supplied pattern
reason: Fixed: safe_regex now takes case_insensitive bool parameter, callers opt in explicitly

reason: Fixed: error message now says bytes instead of characters to match the actual check

title: area filter is implemented with the same matches_entity() path used for generic text pattern filter
reason: Fixed: renamed --area to --name to honestly reflect it is a text match on entity_id/friendly_name, not HA area metadata (which requires websocket API)

title: api_timeout parsed without range or finiteness validation
reason: Added validation for finite and positive values

title: Relative-time branch can overflow and panic with extreme i64 values
reason: Switched to try_ constructors and checked_sub_signed

title: Parser identifies relative expressions with broad contains(ago)
reason: Tightened to exact 3-token structure matching

title: Test module does not exercise risky branches
reason: Added edge case tests for overflow, malformed ago, unsupported units

title: Error impl does not expose source for Other variant
reason: Added source() implementation

title: No From<anyhow::Error> conversion
reason: Added From impl

title: Timeout errors reported with hardcoded 0.0
reason: Stored timeout_secs on struct, used in error

title: Non-JSON error body discarded for 4xx/5xx
reason: Read raw text first, try JSON detail, fall back to raw body

title: Global AtomicBool spinner interferes across instances
reason: Switched to Arc<AtomicBool> per instance

title: Add subcommand does not require any text content
reason: Added required and num_args to clap derive

title: cmd_add ignores result of create_reminder
reason: Now propagates error with warning, only prints success on actual success

title: Tag parsing preserves empty entries
reason: Added filter for empty strings

title: Multiple layers hard-code .js extensions when importing TypeScript sources
reason: Standard ESM TypeScript with moduleResolution:nodenext. .js extensions required by Node.js ESM spec. All 1101 tests pass.

title: Both src/services/mem0.ts and src/services/session-queue.ts declare identifiers that shadow the imported utils namespace
reason: No utils namespace exists in these files or in the diff. Hallucinated finding.

title: episode-store.ts store method iterates and calls upsert individually
reason: Fixed by batching all points into single Qdrant upsert call.

title: broken import that will prevent the server from starting
reason: Standard ESM TypeScript .js import extensions. tsc --noEmit compiles clean, all 1101 tests pass, server runs in production.

title: context.rs only accepts data as top-level array, lost data.results fallback
reason: Fixed by restoring .pointer(/data/results) fallback and client-side truncate(limit).

title: session-episodes.ts nested objects lack additionalProperties:false
reason: Fixed by adding additionalProperties:false to chunk, message, and metadata schemas.

title: session-queue.ts preFilterForGraphIngestion changes semantics
reason: Intentional design - pre-filtering strips noise before fact extraction. Documented in evaluation as the biggest quick win.

title: SQL injection in login query via username interpolation
reason: Classic SQL injection via f-string interpolation

title: SQL injection in admin token lookup
reason: Classic SQL injection via f-string interpolation

title: Hardcoded secret key in source code
reason: SECRET_KEY should be loaded from env var

title: Application runs with Flask debug mode enabled
reason: debug=True exposes Werkzeug debugger in production

title: Database connections are never closed
reason: Resource leak, should use context manager or with statement

title: Database connections are never closed in admin endpoint
reason: Resource leak, should use context manager or with statement

title: Use of direct form indexing can raise unhandled KeyError
reason: Should use request.form.get() with default

title: Threshold is not validated, allowing all findings to collapse into one
reason: threshold=0 would merge everything

title: The test sessions_ingest_transcript_filters_credentials does not effectively verify that credentials are filtered
reason: Fixed by adding body content assertions via server.received_requests()

title: The test case waits for a fixed duration of 200ms using setTimeout
reason: Fixed by replacing setTimeout(200) with vi.waitFor on the actual assertion

title: @fastify/cors is registered with no options, which defaults to origin *
reason: Pre-existing code, not introduced by our diff. Internal API behind VPN, not internet-facing.

title: process.env values are accessed directly without validation
reason: Pre-existing pattern, not introduced by our diff. Env vars validated at startup by existing config layer.

title: The import statement resolves to a path that does not exist (ENOENT)
reason: ESM .js extension imports are standard for TypeScript ESM projects. The build compiles .ts to .js, so the import path is correct at runtime.

title: collect_import_refs_in_range ignores both the AST node and the changed-line bounds
reason: This finding is about third-opinion internal code, not our diff. Wrong file scope.

title: function_def_kinds includes method_definition but collect_calls_in_range resolves callees only via child_by_field_name
reason: This finding is about third-opinion internal code, not our diff.

title: extract_imported_names uses simple string splitting for Rust imports
reason: This finding is about third-opinion internal code, not the reviewed diff.

title: After one full-tree pass in collect_definitions, hydrate traverses the entire tree again
reason: This finding is about third-opinion internal code, not the reviewed diff.

title: Duplicate similar feedback entries can disproportionately suppress or boost findings
reason: Real issue - duplicate feedback entries inflate counts. Should deduplicate by finding signature.

title: Partial and Wontfix verdicts are ignored in suppression/boost logic
reason: Real design gap - partial/wontfix should influence calibration decisions, not just be annotated

title: Word similarity is case-sensitive, reducing match accuracy
reason: SQL Injection vs sql injection would fail to match - should normalize to lowercase

title: Changed type definitions duplicate imported names and will not compile
reason: FP - CalibrationResult and CalibratorConfig ARE defined here, this IS their home module

title: Use of block_in_place can panic on current-thread Tokio runtimes
reason: Real bug - block_in_place panics on current_thread runtime. Should check runtime flavor or document requirement.

title: Potential panic when truncating non-UTF-8-boundary error text
reason: Real bug - &error_text[..200] panics on multi-byte char boundary. Use chars().take() instead.

title: HTTP client lacks request timeout configuration
reason: Real issue - no timeout means hung requests. Should add connect + request timeout.

title: No validation of base_url may allow insecure or malformed endpoint configuration
reason: Config::load already validates base_url with URL parsing and HTTPS enforcement

title: Case-sensitive AWS access key detection misses valid keys
reason: ASIA prefix for temporary STS credentials not covered

title: Overly broad key/value redaction can corrupt non-secret content
reason: Real issue - auth= in URLs or prose can trigger false redaction

title: Private key regex may fail on mixed-case or uncommon PEM labels
reason: PEM standard requires uppercase BEGIN/END. Mixed-case is not valid PEM.

title: URL credential redaction only handles simple usernames
reason: Percent-encoded delimiters in URLs can bypass the regex

title: Cache hit detection is racy and can report false positives/negatives under concurrent requests
reason: Real concurrency bug - global counter compared before/after is not atomic per-request

title: Feedback store load failures are silently ignored
reason: Should log the error, silent degradation hides operational problems

title: Internal error details are exposed to clients
reason: Should return generic 500 and log details server-side

title: Server does not enforce localhost binding despite documentation claim
reason: Binding is enforced in main.rs run_daemon() which hardcodes 127.0.0.1

title: User-controlled file path is reflected verbatim in error response
reason: Could inject control characters. Should sanitize or use generic message.

title: Unbounded prompt injection via raw documentation content formatting
reason: Real concern - Context7 docs could contain prompt injection. Should wrap in fenced blocks.

title: Duplicate framework inputs cause redundant fetches
reason: domain::detect_domain already returns deduplicated HashSet-based results

title: Formatting allows Markdown/header injection through library names
reason: Library names are hardcoded in framework_queries match, not user-controlled

title: Broken OpenAI client initialization and redacted placeholders make fallback pipeline non-functional
reason: Critical - broken code path in production pipeline

title: SSRF risk from downloading arbitrary candidate URLs without validation
reason: Real - scraper downloads user-discovered URLs without origin checks

title: Browser tabs can leak on exceptions in fetch_with_nodriver
reason: Real resource leak - tabs not closed in exception paths

title: Server-side request forgery risk from unvalidated image URLs
reason: Real SSRF - server fetches arbitrary user-provided URLs

title: Using Flask's built-in server in debug mode is unsafe for production
reason: Real - Flask debug mode in production OCR server

title: Redacted API key lines leave setup_dspy_environment syntactically broken
reason: Critical - our secret redaction may have broken this code path in the review output, but the finding about broken initialization is real

title: Disabling web security weakens browser isolation
reason: Real security issue - disable_web_security=True on a scraper visiting arbitrary sites

title: Event loop target is started incorrectly in background thread
reason: Real - asyncio.set_event_loop not called in thread, can cause issues with get_running_loop

title: Blocking future.result() in Scrapy callback can stall the reactor
reason: Real concurrency issue - blocks Twisted reactor for up to 60s per request

title: Broad bare except blocks suppress real failures
reason: Real - multiple bare excepts hide failures in verification, age gate, shutdown

title: Closing logic skips stopping the event loop when browser was never created
reason: Real resource leak - loop thread left running if browser creation fails

title: Logger instance shadows Scrapy's built-in spider logger
reason: Real - overrides Scrapy's logger, loses framework logging integration

title: Cloudflare detection is case-sensitive and fragile
reason: Real - Cloudflare pages may use different casing

title: Unused imports increase noise
reason: Real - urljoin unused at module scope, duplicate random imports

title: No input validation on image URL schemes
reason: Real SSRF - accepts arbitrary URLs including file:// and internal IPs

title: Service enables Flask debug mode from runtime configuration
reason: Critical - debug mode in production OCR service

title: Model fallback check compares against wrong identifier
reason: Real logic bug - comparing wrong model name in fallback

title: Singleton initialization is not thread-safe
reason: Real - concurrent Scrapy pipelines could create multiple instances

title: Selenium WebDriver is launched inside spider callback, blocking the reactor
reason: Critical - launches full browser in Scrapy callback, blocks Twisted reactor

title: 403 retry path does not actually disable duplicate filtering
reason: Real logic bug - dont_filter=True intended but not working as expected

title: Undefined background task function `_auto_analyze_image` causes runtime failure
reason: Function is defined at line 333 in the full file. Quorum only reviewed partial code submission.

title: Missing import for `FileResponse` breaks blob retrieval endpoint
reason: FileResponse is imported on line 10 of the full file via `from fastapi.responses import FileResponse`. Quorum only reviewed partial code.

title: Unbounded prompt construction can exceed model limits and amplify latency/cost
reason: Real issue. Large captures + many graph relations can blow past token limits. Should add truncation.

title: Graph relation formatting can raise KeyError if relation dicts are incomplete
reason: Valid — upstream graph service could return partial data. Should use .get() with defaults.

title: Potential duplicate graph relations degrade answer quality and inflate prompt size
reason: Real issue. Relations from multiple sources get appended without dedup. Should deduplicate.

title: LLM response access can crash on empty or malformed completions
reason: Valid. Should handle empty choices or None content defensively.

title: Sensitive secrets are stored as plain strings instead of SecretStr
reason: Good practice to use SecretStr for passwords and API keys to prevent accidental log exposure.

title: Mutable list used as class-level default for CORS origins
reason: Pydantic BaseModel/BaseSettings copies defaults per instance — mutable default sharing is not an issue here unlike plain Python classes.

title: Unbounded user input may enable resource exhaustion
reason: Should add max_length to ChatRequest.message to prevent abuse.

title: Database session lifetime may extend for entire SSE stream
reason: Valid concern. Should do DB work before streaming starts.

title: Broad exception catch masks cancellation/disconnect behavior
reason: Should re-raise asyncio.CancelledError instead of catching it.

title: Error logging drops stack trace information
reason: Should use exc_info=True or logger.exception() for debugging.

title: Missing SSE cache-control headers may cause proxy/client buffering issues
reason: Should add Cache-Control: no-cache and X-Accel-Buffering: no headers.

title: Inbox listing fetches all records into memory before pagination
reason: Real issue. Should push pagination to DB queries instead of in-memory slicing.

title: Pagination parameters are not validated, allowing negative or excessive values
reason: Should add Query(ge=0, le=100) constraints.

title: Sorting by ISO-formatted strings instead of datetimes is fragile
reason: Should sort by actual datetime objects before converting to ISO strings.

title: Synchronous `memory.add` call inside async endpoint can block event loop
reason: Should wrap in run_in_executor like the ask_service does for graph calls.

title: List endpoint does not validate or constrain pagination inputs
reason: Should add Query constraints like the search endpoint does.

title: Unused dependency parameter `graph_service` indicates dead code
reason: Should remove unused dependency injection.

title: Unused form field `description` in blob upload endpoint
reason: Dead parameter, should remove.

title: Out-of-bounds slice panic when start_line exceeds file length
reason: Real panic - start_idx not clamped to lines.len()

title: Recursive grep can escape repository root through symlinked directories
reason: Real security issue - symlinks not checked during traversal

title: Configuration limits are defined but never enforced
reason: Real - max_iterations/max_tool_calls/max_bytes not used, agent is single-pass MVP

title: Tool registry is only used for prompt text and not for actual tool execution
reason: Correct - v0.5 MVP, real tool calling loop is next iteration

title: Duplicate AgentConfig definition
reason: FP - AgentConfig is only defined here, not duplicated

title: Relay is configured to always turn on after reboot
reason: Intentional: refrigerator relay MUST default on after power loss to keep food safe

title: Relay restore mode 'ALWAYS_ON' can cause unexpected activation
reason: Same as above -- intentional for refrigerator appliance monitoring

title: Fallback AP is configured without captive_portal component
reason: Fallback AP activates only when WiFi fails -- that's its intended purpose. Not a security issue.

title: Boot-strap pin GPIO0 is used as a button input
reason: Standard Sonoff/Tuya ESP8266 design. Every off-the-shelf relay board uses GPIO0 for button.

title: Hard-coded sunrise and sunset times break true circadian behavior
reason: Intentional for ADHD-friendly consistent scheduling. Not a bug.

title: Hard-coded sunrise and sunset times will drift from actual daylight conditions
reason: Same as above -- intentional design choice for predictable lighting schedule.

title: Template list accumulation inside loop will not persist
reason: Real bug: Jinja loop scoping. Need namespace() to accumulate across iterations.

title: Leaderboard renders negative winning scores with a malformed plus sign
reason: Real formatting bug, easy fix.

title: Malformed or missing AI output is treated as a real litter visit
reason: Real bug: should fallback to no_detection or skip counting on malformed LLM response.

title: Fresh-air suggestion unreachable when all windows closed
reason: Real logic bug in condition ordering.

title: Stale device list attribute is serialized as a string, breaking report iteration
reason: Real bug: string serialization breaks downstream template iteration.

title: Trigger-based template sensors do not initialize on Home Assistant startup
reason: Real bug: need homeassistant start trigger for PID sensors.

title: Unmapped behaviors are never logged if they score as zero
reason: Real bug: defeats the purpose of tracking unmapped behaviors.

title: Misleading notification can report Both Dogs/Cats when only one pet had a negative event
reason: Real logic error in notification template.

title: CO2 control comparisons use float(0) fallbacks, which can turn fans off on sensor unavailability
reason: Real safety issue: unavailable sensors treated as 0 CO2, suppressing ventilation.

title: Sensor failures are treated as safe values and can incorrectly suppress ventilation
reason: Same root cause as above -- real safety concern.

title: Basement CO2 control also treats unavailable readings as zero
reason: Same pattern, different sensor. Real bug.

title: homeassistant.debug: false key
reason: Silently ignored by HA, no validation failure. Not a real bug.

title: Fallback AP enabled permanently
reason: That's literally how ESPHome fallback AP works - only activates when WiFi fails

title: GPIO15 on front-porch-air-quality
reason: Not present in this file - false positive, wrong file referenced

title: Shower-ended humidity recheck is incorrect
reason: Logic is correct: shower_ended checks threshold at entry

title: Basement shower not triggering fan immediately
reason: Shower activity IS highest-priority choose branch. Triggers correctly.

title: HVAC re-enable watching hvac_mode attribute
reason: Valid trigger: climate entities expose hvac_mode as attribute. Works correctly.

title: wifi.domain .5745.house
reason: Correct DNS domain for network. Not mDNS .local issue.

title: Daily energy depends on HA time
reason: Standard ESPHome pattern using homeassistant.time. Correct approach.

title: Cross-package helper dependencies
reason: Defined in core config, working as designed. Not undeclared.

title: Unused homeassistant_time
reason: Confirmed used by total_daily_energy sensor. Not unused.

title: Using `StreamReader.read(CHUNK_BYTES)` can return partial frames and break PCM decoding
reason: asyncio StreamReader.read() can return fewer bytes than requested. Should use readexactly() or accumulate to CHUNK_BYTES. This could cause numpy frombuffer to fail or produce misaligned PCM frames.

title: go2rtc-only cameras may connect directly to the camera instead of the proxy
reason: If go2rtc fallback fails, the original RTSP URL (direct to camera) is used. This bypasses the proxy and can consume a camera's limited RTSP session slots. The backyard camera config relies on go2rtc_stream with no direct fallback intended.

title: Calling `start()` multiple times spawns duplicate stream tasks
reason: The StreamManager._run() method is only called once from create_app's startup handler. There's no code path that calls start() multiple times.

title: Background LLM judge tasks are not tracked or cancelled on shutdown
reason: Fire-and-forget LLM judge tasks could be mid-HTTP-request when the addon shuts down, causing warnings. Should be tracked and cancelled in shutdown handler.

title: Discovery silently ignores `ScryptedUrlResolver` despite accepting it in the type signature
reason: ScryptedApiResolver is the preferred resolver and is used first. ScryptedUrlResolver (go2rtc-based) is used as a fallback in the stream loop, not in _attempt_discovery. The type signature correctly reflects both pathways.

title: Unified search omits the user_id tenant filter
reason: Real gap — searchUnified was missing the user_id filter that other search methods had. Fixed by adding tenant filter to must array.

title: getPoint retrieves arbitrary IDs without validating ownership
reason: Valid security finding. Added user_id ownership check on returned point payload.

title: getCollectionName fallback points to a different default collection than config creation
reason: Correct — fallback was 'house_memory' but production uses 'house_memory_v2'. Would cause silent data misses if config was absent.

title: Unbounded `limit` query parameter can trigger expensive searches
reason: Valid — no max was enforced. Capped at 100 with Math.min().

title: Server startup hard-requires OPENROUTER_API_KEY despite fallback logic for other API keys
reason: Real bug — requireEnv would throw even when OPENAI_API_KEY was set. Fixed with either-or fallback.

title: Unescaped memory/project content can break hook context framing
reason: Valid prompt injection vector — pipes and angle brackets in memory content could corrupt the markdown table and XML wrapper fed to LLM context. Fixed with sanitization.

title: Missing validation for `chunk_size` may trigger panics or invalid chunking behavior
reason: Valid — chunk_size=0 could cause division-by-zero in chunk_exchanges. Added early validation.

title: Test does not validate the real route definition
reason: Correct — test only asserted against a local object literal, not the actual Fastify route. Rewrote with proper injection tests.

title: getPoint masks backend failures by converting all errors to null
reason: Valid — swallowing errors silently made debugging impossible. Added error logging while keeping null return for callers.

title: Timestamp parser is stricter than the documented/expected input format
reason: Backend always returns RFC3339 timestamps with timezone. The parser matches the actual data format correctly.

title: Global authentication hook may unintentionally protect health and readiness endpoints
reason: createAuthMiddleware already exempts /health — this is handled in the middleware implementation.

title: Session transcript ingestion via positional CLI argument can leak sensitive content
reason: The positional arg is a file path, not the transcript content itself. Content is read from the file.

title: Privacy filter not used in the ingestion path
reason: strip_privacy_tags IS called in sessions.rs before ingestion (line 118). The classify functions operate on already-stripped content in the pipeline.

title: Session locking is not actually atomic and allows duplicate concurrent processing
reason: Fixed with UPDATE...RETURNING to atomically claim and return rows in one query.

title: Database row is marked enriched before embedding and Qdrant update complete
reason: Fixed by reordering: Qdrant upsert now happens before PG status update to enriched.

title: Unknown LLM item IDs are silently treated as successful enrichments
reason: Fixed: applyEnrichment now throws on unknown IDs instead of returning early.

title: Model output validation does not verify facts and concepts contain strings
reason: Fixed: added .every(f => typeof f === 'string') validation for both arrays.

title: Items retried by moving back to pending are counted as failures
reason: Fixed: now uses skipped counter for retriable items, failed only for permanent failures.

title: Unbounded fire-and-forget execution can exhaust server resources
reason: Endpoint is behind auth middleware and only called by the session-end hook (one call per session). The atomic UPDATE...RETURNING ensures concurrent calls for the same session_id are safe — second caller gets 0 rows. Not exposed to untrusted clients.

title: Querystring schema does not reject unexpected parameters
reason: Standard Fastify convention. Extra query params are harmless and ignored. No other route in this codebase uses additionalProperties:false on querystrings.

title: enrichment_status can still be NULL despite having a default and CHECK constraint
reason: Fixed: added NOT NULL to the column definition.

title: Test claims to verify PG and Qdrant updates but never asserts those side effects
reason: Fixed: added assertions for mockQdrant.upsert and mockOpenAI.embeddings.create call counts.

title: Unused imported type adds noise and suggests stale test code
reason: Fixed: removed unused EnrichmentResult import.

title: Shared mock state can leak between tests
reason: Fixed: added beforeEach with mockReset() for the shared mock.

title: Test does not verify that async compression was actually triggered
reason: Fixed: added setImmediate await + assertion that compressSession was called with the expected session_id.

title: Unsanitized session ID is used in a /tmp path
reason: Claude Code session IDs are always UUIDs (hex + hyphens). No path traversal possible.

title: Predictable /tmp counter file is vulnerable to symlink and tampering attacks
reason: Single-user dev machine, not multi-tenant. Counter is informational only — tampered value has zero security impact.

title: Observation data is sent over plaintext HTTP by default
reason: Local network traffic to Docker VM on same subnet. All other hooks and services in this project use the same HTTP endpoint. HTTPS is unnecessary for LAN-only traffic.

title: Rate limit file in /tmp is vulnerable to symlink clobbering
reason: Single-user dev machine. Worst case a symlink causes rate limit to misfire — one extra or skipped observation. Zero security impact.

title: Unused LOG_FILE variable indicates dead code or incomplete logging
reason: Fixed — removed the unused variable.

title: Authentication header may be sent over insecure HTTP by default
reason: Same as tool-observe — local network to Docker VM. All services use HTTP on LAN.

title: Idempotency check is race-prone and can trigger duplicate ingestions
reason: Fixed — added .ingesting lock file created before backgrounding, removed after completion.

title: session_id is interpolated into URL without encoding
reason: Fixed — now URL-encodes via jq @uri before interpolation.

title: Shared `app` instance is not cleared after teardown
reason: Minor but real — should set app = undefined after close.

title: Leak detection may be permanently disabled by checking a non-existent statistics attribute
reason: source_value_valid is a real HA statistics sensor attribute. Confirmed it exists on sensor.water_flow_rate_1hr_avg (currently false with age_coverage_ratio 0.64). The guard is working as intended to prevent false alarms on stale data.

title: Template condition can fail when `event_type` is missing
reason: Valid concern. During HA startup/restore, event entities may have incomplete attributes. Using attributes.get('event_type') would be more defensive.

title: Input boolean forces enabled state on every restart
reason: Valid. If someone disables buttons intentionally, a restart would silently re-enable them. Removing initial: true lets HA restore the previous state.

title: Lock file creation is non-atomic and allows concurrent checkpoint runs
reason: Real issue. Changed from touch to mkdir for atomic locking. Also caught by Gemini 3.1 Pro review.

title: Full-directory find on every hook invocation can violate the never block goal
reason: Real performance issue. Replaced find with direct path construction from CWD, with heartbeat cache fallback.

title: Lock file is leaked on early exits, blocking future ingestion for the session
reason: Real bug. Gates 4 and 5 exited without removing the .ingesting lock file, permanently blocking re-ingestion. Added rm -f before early exits.

title: Predictable /tmp filenames derived from untrusted session_id enable file clobbering/symlink attacks
reason: Valid security concern. Moved all state files from /tmp to ~/.claude/housemem-run/ which is user-owned and restricted.

title: Predictable files in /tmp create symlink and race-condition risks
reason: Same as session-summary finding. Moved all state to ~/.claude/housemem-run/.

title: Duplicate `SearchArgs` definition can cause type mismatch and maintenance issues
reason: This IS the canonical definition. main.rs imports it via `use housemem::commands::search::SearchArgs`. There is no duplication.

title: Function `run` has cyclomatic complexity 5
reason: Complexity 5 equals threshold (not exceeds). The function is just optional parameter building — straightforward and readable.

title: No validation on `limit` allows ineffective or invalid requests
reason: This is a thin CLI client. Server-side validation is the right boundary. Adding client-side validation duplicates logic and creates drift risk.

title: Unvalidated date/time range parameters may allow invalid searches
reason: Same as limit — server validates. Thin CLI client should not duplicate server validation logic.

title: Manual query string construction is error-prone
reason: Values are URL-encoded via urlencoding::encode. The construction is simple, correct, and avoids adding a URL builder dependency for 5 params.

title: No CLI-level bounds checking for chunk_size and max_exchanges
reason: Fixed by adding clap value_parser with range(1..) constraints. Zero values would cause division-by-zero or empty iteration downstream. Also removed now-redundant runtime check.

title: Sensitive error details are always emitted to stderr as JSON
reason: AppError variants are designed for CLI output — they contain error codes and user-facing messages, not raw request context or credentials. The JSON format is intentional for machine-parseable error handling.

title: CLI help claims environment variable support but arguments are not wired to Clap env parsing
reason: auth::resolve_auth intentionally handles env var resolution with multi-variable fallback (HOUSEMEM_URL, HOUSEMEM_API_URL) and TOML config file support. Clap env= can't express this logic.

title: Date filter arguments are accepted as arbitrary strings without validation
reason: Duplicate of search.rs finding. Thin CLI client — server validates date formats. Client-side parsing would create coupling to a specific format the server might evolve.

title: Arrow function async detection uses unsafe and inaccurate text heuristics
reason: Confirmed: contains("async") on grandparent text matched variable names like asyncHandler, causing false positives. Fixed by checking for "async" child node in AST instead of text heuristics.

title: Python `open()` binary-mode detection is incomplete
reason: Confirmed: xb and r+b modes were missing. Fixed by expanding binary_modes array to include xb, r+b, w+b, a+b, x+b and their + variants for both quote styles.

title: Function `is_in_async_function` has cyclomatic complexity 9
reason: Valid. The function was overly complex due to separate text-based heuristics for each function type. After refactoring to AST-based async detection, the function is simpler with a single unified check across all function types.

title: Bare-except detection uses `starts_with` on node source and is whitespace-sensitive
reason: Confirmed fragile. Rewrote to use AST child iteration — walks named children for identifier/as_pattern to get exception type, and finds block child for body. Much more robust than text matching.

title: Rule title overstates scope for `except Exception: pass`
reason: Fixed title from "Bare except: pass silently swallows all errors" to "Catch-all except: pass silently swallows errors" and removed specific KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit claim from description.

title: Async-function detection relies on direct child kind `async`, which is grammar-fragile
reason: The "async" token IS a named child in tree-sitter-typescript grammar. All 6 async tests pass including exported async, arrow, and method forms. The claim that async may be an anonymous token is incorrect for this grammar.

title: Tautological `.length >= 0` rule is based on text suffix instead of property access AST
reason: Valid improvement. Replaced ends_with(".length") text check with member_expression kind check + child_by_field_name("property") == "length". More precise AST-based detection.

title: Empty TypeScript catch-block detection may treat non-empty blocks as empty
reason: The claim that comments may not be named children is incorrect for tree-sitter-typescript — comments ARE named nodes (extras). The filtering logic correctly handles this, as verified by 5 passing tests including comment-only and empty_statement cases.

title: Silent error suppression hides ast-grep execution and parsing failures
reason: All 3 reviewers (GPT-5.4, Gemini 3 Pro, quorum) flagged this. Err(_) => continue hides broken rules and tool failures, making them indistinguishable from clean results.

title: Rule file collection ignores `.yaml` files despite YAML extension support elsewhere
reason: Real bug - users commonly use .yaml extension for rule files. Both collect_rule_files and ast_grep_has_rules need to accept both.

title: Wrong language mapping for JavaScript files routes JS rules to TypeScript directory
reason: Intentional design - ast-grep uses the TypeScript grammar to parse JS files. All TS rules apply to JS too. Worth a comment but not a bug.

title: Function `ext_to_ast_grep_lang` has cyclomatic complexity 7
reason: Flat match statement mapping extensions to language names. Not actually complex - each arm is trivial.

title: No URL scheme/host validation on extracted hrefs before following with Request()
reason: Third-opinion and PAL both caught this. urljoin'd URLs from scraped HTML are followed without validating scheme (could be non-http) or host (could go off-domain). allowed_domains doesn't protect manually created Requests unless OffsiteMiddleware is enabled.

title: Event loop shutdown race - parse_with_nodriver submits work to loop that may be closed
reason: PAL and third-opinion caught this. In closed(), the loop is stopped then closed, but callbacks may still be in-flight. run_coroutine_threadsafe can raise if the loop is closed. Need loop state check or shutdown flag.

title: Browser tabs are leaked when fetch_with_nodriver fails before normal close
reason: All 3 tools caught this. Tab created with new_tab() only closed on success path. Exceptions after tab creation leak browser tabs. Need finally block.

title: Scrapy callback blocks while waiting on asyncio future
reason: Quorum and PAL caught this. future.result(timeout=60) blocks Twisted reactor for up to 60s per request, defeating Scrapy concurrency.

title: Spider performs duplicate network fetches for every URL
reason: All 3 tools caught this. No deduplication of expensive nodriver requests. dont_filter=True plus no visited set means same URL can be fetched multiple times via browser.

title: disable_web_security weakens browser isolation and can alter site behavior
reason: Quorum and PAL caught this. Disabling web security broadens cross-origin access unnecessarily for COA scraping.

title: Event listener is never unsubscribed because `.bind(this)` creates a new function
reason: Real pattern issue. The ECS plugin already fixed this by storing bound references. Biomes, crafting, daynight, interaction, particles, sound plugins all have the same bug.

title: Event listeners are never removed because bound handler references differ
reason: Same .bind(this) pattern issue as biomes plugin. ECS plugin shows the correct pattern with stored bound references.

title: Event listener is not removed because `bind(this)` creates a different function reference
reason: Same .bind(this) leak pattern across all plugins.

title: Update listener cannot be removed because `bind()` creates a new function
reason: Same .bind(this) leak pattern.

title: Event listeners cannot be removed because `.bind(this)` creates new function instances
reason: Same .bind(this) leak pattern.

title: Event listeners cannot be removed because `.bind(this)` creates new function objects
reason: Same .bind(this) leak pattern.

title: Engine can be left in a broken running state if startup async work fails
reason: start() sets running=true before awaiting enableAll(). If that rejects, running stays true but animation loop never started. Real state management bug.

title: Chunk completion path is not exception-safe and can strand jobs as permanently busy
reason: If onmessage handler throws after receiving chunk data, the worker stays marked busy forever. Real resource leak.

title: Errors during async world generation leave the promise unresolved and player state inconsistent
reason: initializeWorld has no try/catch around generateInitialTerrain/updateChunkMeshes. If they throw, player stays frozen at y=120 and promise never resolves.

title: ASCII parser strips leading and trailing spaces from every row
reason: If pixel art uses spaces for transparent/white pixels at row edges, trim() removes them, corrupting the texture. Real rendering bug.
