reason: Real test-strength gap. The original test had only one query so query-order stability was unobservable. Rewrote to use two queries with non-alphabetical first-seen order, and asserted both query sequence and per-query doc sequence match across repeated calls.

title: Cache writes are non-atomic and can leave corrupted files behind
reason: Real reliability gap. A crash mid-fs::write would leave a truncated file that subsequent `get` would reject as corrupt, failing the eval run. Replaced with write-to-sibling-tmp + rename pattern (PID-suffixed tmp to avoid concurrent-run collisions).

title: Cache key hashing is ambiguous for inputs containing NUL bytes
reason: Low probability but real ambiguity. Switched from NUL-separator to length-prefixed concatenation (u64-LE length per field), which is unambiguous for any byte input and locks in a stable on-disk format. Added known-vector + boundary-ambiguity tests.

title: Hash test does not actually verify SHA-256 or deterministic layout
reason: Real gap. Added key_hash_matches_known_vector with a fixture computed independently in Python. Any future change to hash derivation will fail this test and force an explicit decision about orphaning existing cache files.

title: Search results with missing or non-string `id` fields are silently discarded
reason: Same class of silent-failure as the data-not-array finding. Replaced filter_map with explicit loop that errors with (query, variant, index) context when any row has missing/non-string id. Added regression test.

title: Error-path tests do not verify that invalid LLM outputs are not cached
reason: Real invariant that was untested. Added judge_does_not_cache_on_parse_error — scans the cache dir after a bad-grade LLM reply and asserts it's empty. Impl was already correct (parse_grade runs before cache.put) but the guarantee is now locked in.

title: Cache-miss test validates only one small fragment of the outbound request
reason: Real gap — the original `body_partial_json({"temperature": 0})` would pass even if model, rubric, query, or doc were wrong. Tightened to assert model + temperature + rubric-in-system-message, plus a post-call received_requests check for query + doc content in the user prompt.

title: Cached judgments are reused even when the rubric or judge model changes
reason: Misread. The EvalCacheKey hash input includes both rubric_version and judge_snapshot, so changing either produces a different cache slot and a miss. The `every_component_affects_the_key` test in eval_cache.rs proves this for all 5 components including these two.

title: Cached metadata is not validated against the requested key on read
reason: Defense-in-depth over sha256 — a mismatch can only happen if the cache file was manually edited or sha256 collided. The former is operator error (out of scope); the latter has cosmic odds. Adding validation would add noise without preventing realistic failure modes.

title: Failed error-body reads are swallowed with an empty default string
reason: Affects only the quality of an error message when the upstream status is already non-2xx. The AppError::Http still reports the status code, which is the actionable signal. Trading complexity for a rarely-useful error string is net-negative here.

title: LiteLLM judge requests have no timeout and can hang an entire eval run
reason: Real reliability gap. reqwest::Client::new() has no default timeout, so a hung LiteLLM upstream would stall the whole eval. Replaced with an explicitly-built client carrying a 60s timeout.

title: Default rubric test can pass even if the grading scale is missing or incorrect
reason: Real weak assertion. `prompt.contains('0')` is trivially true for any prose. Strengthened to assert each grade label ('0 —' through '3 —') appears verbatim plus the "one digit" instruction that matches the strict parser — rubric drift that dropped a grade would now fail the test.

title: Test panics on non-UTF-8 temporary paths instead of exercising `load_rubric`
reason: Test-code idiom. NamedTempFile paths on macOS/Linux CI are UTF-8. Same class as earlier UTF-8 tempfile findings — real concern would be production API, not the test helper.

title: Rubric does not define behavior for empty or whitespace-only queries
reason: Wrong layer. The eval query file format (Task 5.3) reads a JSONL where each row must have a non-empty `query` string — blank queries can't reach the judge. Adding rubric prose to cover that case would bloat the prompt without changing behavior.

title: Cached judgments are reused even when the judge model or rubric changes
reason: Third time quorum has raised this. EvalCacheKey includes both judge_snapshot and rubric_version; changing either produces a distinct hash and a cache miss. The `every_component_affects_the_key` test in eval_cache.rs asserts this directly for all 5 components. Recording as FP again to keep the calibrator informed.

title: Use of `.unwrap()` may panic at runtime
reason: Test-helper idiom. Tempfile creation `.unwrap()` inside a test body is standard Rust testing — failure means test infrastructure is broken, not that production code has a bug.

title: Prompt-content assertion is too weak to catch missing per-item context
reason: Real gap — `contains("excerpt")` and `contains("grade")` would pass even if the prompt dropped the query text or doc_id. Replaced with specific-value assertions: query text, each of three doc_ids, and all three distinct "Cached grade: N" lines.

title: Invalid-input test can pass even if no re-prompt or validation message is emitted
reason: Classic accidentally-trivial assertion. `contains("y") && contains("n")` matches the "Query:" label and any prose with those letters. Tightened to check for the specific "Please answer" / "y/n" guidance string AND the "skipping" notice that proves the re-prompt-then-skip path actually executed.

title: No regression tests cover `k == 0` for DCG/NDCG/Recall edge handling
reason: Real gap. k=0 is a degenerate but legal input; leaving it untested would let a future refactor that panics or returns NaN slip through. Added k_zero_returns_zero_for_all_metrics.

title: Recall tests are logically unsound because they infer document matches from grades
reason: Mismatch of test layer vs pipeline layer. The metric API is grade-based by design — `ranked_grades[i]` is the grade for whatever doc the variant put at rank i, looked up in the judged pool. Tests exercise the metric math directly; pipeline-level ID correspondence is tested by run_variant_sweep + pool_candidates. Conflating the two layers in one test would obscure both.

title: Temporary cache file name can collide across concurrent writes from the same process
reason: Real concurrency bug. Two concurrent put() calls on the same key in the same process shared a PID-based tmp path. The first writer's rename could remove the file out from under the second writer. Today's sweep is sequential but a parallel judge executor is on the roadmap. Fixed by replacing the PID scheme with tempfile::NamedTempFile::new_in, which uses a random suffix.

title: Cache reads can observe partially written files because writes are not durably flushed before rename
reason: Valid durability gap. fs::write + fs::rename makes the name visible before data blocks are necessarily synced. On crash/power-loss the final path could resolve to a zero-length file that get() then has to quarantine as corrupt. Fixed by explicitly calling sync_all() on the tmp file between write and rename.

title: Function can panic on NaN inputs during bootstrap sample sorting
reason: Real input-validation gap. NaN in a[] or b[] propagates to per-iteration means; the sort's partial_cmp then panics via the expect() call. Fixed by rejecting any non-finite value (NaN or ±inf) at the top of the function with a clear AppError. Added two tests: nan_in_inputs_is_an_error and infinite_inputs_are_an_error. RED→GREEN confirmed.

title: Calibration may repeatedly hit cache instead of re-judging, masking nondeterminism across passes
reason: Misread the code path. calibrate_judge calls call_judge_direct, which is the cache-bypass path used by audit_judgments — the cache is never read or written in that function. The EvalCacheKey is constructed only because call_judge_direct's signature takes one; call_judge_direct reads only key.query and key.doc_id for the prompt string and ignores the rest. The identical_grades_across_passes test in eval_calibration.rs stages 9 sequential wiremock responses and asserts all 9 HTTP calls happen, proving cache-bypass.

title: Cache key omits document content hash, enabling stale or cross-document judge results
reason: Same root cause as the cache-hit FP. The EvalCacheKey is constructed in calibrate_judge to satisfy call_judge_direct's signature, but call_judge_direct never touches the cache — it reads only key.query and key.doc_id for the prompt. doc_content_hash, judge_snapshot, and rubric_version in the calibration key path are unused. Populating a real doc_content_hash here wouldn't change behaviour because no cache read/write happens.

title: Calibration computes NDCG against the same grades as both predictions and ground truth
reason: The claim "every pass scores a perfect or near-perfect NDCG regardless of judge stability" is factually wrong. ndcg_at_k(grades, grades, k) measures how close the fixed rank order is to the ideal ordering of THIS pass's own grades — it is 1.0 iff the grades are already in descending order. A rank-flipping judge (e.g. grades [0,3] in rank order) gives NDCG = 3/log2(3) / 3 ≈ 0.63, not 1.0. Uniform order-preserving grade drifts (e.g. pass1=[3,2,1], pass2=[2,1,0]) correctly produce NDCG=1 each → zero variance, which IS what we want because such drift doesn't affect A/B ranking. Rank-order flips correctly produce NDCG < 1 → non-zero stddev. The test varying_grades_across_passes_produce_nonzero_stddev explicitly pins this behavior with NDCGs [1.0, 1.0, 0.631] across three passes.

title: Recall metric can be inflated by duplicate document IDs in a variant's ranked list
reason: Real bug. Fixed by deduping each cell's ranked list by doc_id (first occurrence wins) before converting to grades. Added regression test that mounts a variant returning [d1,d1,d1,d2] and asserts recall == 1.0 exactly (was 2.0 pre-fix). Commit ee6cadf.

title: Mean metric computation can produce NaN when a variant has no per-query scores
reason: Unreachable. The code guards pools.is_empty() before the push loop, and for each pool we push one entry into every variant's vector — so per_variant_ndcg[v].len() always equals pools.len() >= 1. No path that bypasses the check exists.

title: Use of `.unwrap()` may panic at runtime
reason: Flagged unwrap is `per_variant_ndcg.get_mut(v).unwrap()` where v iterates the same variants vec we preinserted entries for five lines above. The invariant is enforced by the immediately preceding for loop — unreachable panic.

title: Non-finite numeric inputs bypass validation and can corrupt projection results
reason: Real bug. NaN comparisons silently return false so `NaN <= 0` wouldn't trip the positivity check, disabling the abort gate entirely. Fixed with Number.isFinite + Number.isInteger checks on corpusCount and nonzeroDims, Number.isFinite on abortBytes. Added 8 table-driven tests covering NaN/Infinity/fractional inputs for all three fields. Commit 8be3c9d.

title: Projection arithmetic can exceed JavaScript safe integer precision
reason: Not reachable in practice. Our corpus is 72k docs × max ~1000 nonzero dims × 8 bytes = 5.76e8 bytes, 7 orders of magnitude below Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER (9e15). The integer validation on corpusCount + the Qdrant 250 MiB abort threshold both cap this well before precision matters. Using BigInt here would add complexity without a real-world failure mode.

title: Detached LLM judge tasks are never tracked or cancelled
reason: Real resource lifecycle bug. Fixed by adding _judge_tasks set, done-callback cleanup, and cancel+drain in stop(). Three new passing tests in TestJudgeTaskLifecycle cover the invariants. Commit 664a1a0.

title: Audio reader assumes `read(CHUNK_BYTES)` returns a full PCM chunk
reason: Real bug. StreamReader.read(n) returns at-most-n bytes; partial reads would fail np.frombuffer on odd lengths and skew dB/clip accounting. Fixed by switching to readexactly(n) with IncompleteReadError handling for EOF.

title: Recovery path publishes camera online while the stream is actually offline
reason: Legit bug. The stuck-recovery branch fired publish_camera_online immediately after the finally block had published camera_offline, and before reconnecting. External consumers saw false online state during reconnect window. Removed the redundant publish — the next successful connect publishes online correctly.

title: Credential-redaction regex is malformed and can leak secrets into logs/events
reason: Hallucinated description. The LLM claimed the pattern was `[^:[REDACTED]@]+` but the actual code uses two separate correct character classes: `r"://([^:]+):([^@]+)@"`. The `***@` in the replacement string confused the model into thinking it was part of the match pattern. Regex is correct and redaction works.

title: Credential redaction regex is malformed and can leak RTSP credentials into logs/events
reason: Same hallucination as the stream_manager finding. Actual code uses `r"://([^:]+):([^@]+)@"` which correctly captures username and password groups separately. The `***@` in the replacement string is confusing the LLM. No leak.

title: Stuck-mode fallback can bypass the resolver/proxy and connect directly to the camera
reason: The `self._camera.rtsp_url` IS the user-configured URL from config.yaml, which for Scrypted-backed cameras is the rebroadcast URL, not a direct camera URL. go2rtc was removed in v0.8.6; there's no separate proxy layer. Reverting to configured URL in stuck mode is intentional fallback behavior — it doesn't bypass anything the user didn't configure. No security/architecture regression.

title: Malformed 200 JSON responses are retried instead of failing fast
reason: Real bug. resp.json() throws generic SyntaxError which the outer retry loop treated as transient, defeating the documented fail-fast-on-contract-bug intent. Fixed by wrapping resp.json() in try/catch and re-throwing as ContractError. Added regression test (fetchMock called once, not 3x).

title: Successful responses are not validated beyond top-level array shape
reason: Legitimate defensive gap. A missing `index` or non-finite `value` in a TEI entry would produce { indices: [undefined], values: [NaN] } and fail later at the Qdrant upsert with a less actionable error. Added per-entry Number.isInteger(index) + Number.isFinite(value) validation at the contract boundary, throwing ContractError with entry index + payload excerpt. Added regression test.

title: Duplicate captures for the same query and mode are silently overwritten
reason: Real silent-failure. loadByQuery used Map.set without dup check — a duplicate (mode, query) means capture ran twice and the second overwrites the first, invalidating comparison semantics. Fixed by throwing on duplicate with mode + query + file in the error message.

title: Failed captures are diffed as if they were valid results
reason: Real data-quality issue. A capture with ok:false has an empty results array, which when diffed against a successful capture inflates the "dropped from top-K" count with noise. Fixed by checking both b.ok and a.ok before diffing, counting capturedFailures, and reporting the count in the summary.

title: Summary metrics use the wrong query denominator when captures are missing on one side
reason: Real metric-dilution bug. Summary printed "top-1 flips=N/queries.length" and "mean overlap=avg/10" where queries.length includes skipped (missing + failed) captures, masking the actual flip rate. Fixed to use matched query count (overlaps.length) as the denominator and to explicitly report compared/missing/failed counts separately.

title: Missing regression tests for legal `k == 0` edge case
reason: Cheap, legitimate gap. k=0 exercises the empty-vs-empty Jaccard convention (1.0) and the short-result comparison (len < 0 is impossible so rate must be 0). Added two regression tests asserting finite output and the documented contract values. No production code change needed — tests just lock in behavior.

title: Overlap and short-result metrics silently ignore missing query/variant cells
reason: Unreachable in current code path. compute_variant_overlap / compute_short_result_rates are only called from run_with_config, which receives cells from run_variant_sweep. run_variant_sweep fails loudly on the first HTTP error or missing data/memory field (InvalidResponse), so the input grid is always complete — one cell per (query, variant). The flagged "partial sweep" scenario doesn't exist. Same class as a prior FP on per_variant_ndcg.get_mut(v).unwrap() where the invariant is enforced by immediately-preceding code. Adding a guard would be defensive code for an already-enforced invariant (YAGNI).

title: Use of `.unwrap()` may panic at runtime
reason: Invariant-enforced unwrap. The flagged unwraps are per_variant_ndcg.get_mut(v).unwrap() / per_variant_recall.get_mut(v).unwrap() where `v` iterates the same variants Vec that was used to pre-populate the HashMap entries a few lines above. Unreachable panic. Calibrator already has two prior FP entries at sim=1.00 and sim=0.98 for this exact pattern.

title: Constructor does not validate `baseUrl`, causing pointless retries on invalid configuration
reason: fetch() surfaces a clear, immediate TypeError on malformed URLs ("Failed to parse URL from ..."), which falls through the retry loop as a non-ContractError/non-PermanentHttpError and does retry — but only once per call site startup since it's a config issue caught at first use. The retry overhead (3x * backoff) is ~1.5s, acceptable for a deployment misconfig. Adding URL validation in the constructor is defensive without meaningful risk reduction — config issues surface on first query either way.

title: Non-OK responses fully buffer the body before truncating
reason: TEI error responses are bounded JSON envelopes (a few hundred bytes — {"error":"...","error_type":"..."}), not large streams. Full buffering + slice-to-200 in the error path is negligible cost and produces a cleaner diagnostic message than chunked reading. Not a performance or memory concern at this scale.

title: Function `_stream_loop` has cyclomatic complexity 29
reason: Real complexity. The function handles a lot: dB gating, pre-trigger buffer, clip capture, threshold resolution, ambient tracking, classification dispatch, CLAP verification, confounder tagging, MQTT publishing, LLM judge dispatch. Refactoring is on the roadmap (split pipeline stages) but not in scope for the current stream-reliability work. Accepted debt.

title: Function `_run` has cyclomatic complexity 18
reason: Real complexity. _run is the main per-camera loop managing connect/stream/reconnect/backoff/stuck-detection/fast-reconnect state machine. Refactor target — state machine could be extracted — but stable and tested. Accepted debt.

title: Function `read_audio_clip` has cyclomatic complexity 11
reason: Barely over threshold (11 vs 10). Combines PCM read, dB gating, adaptive threshold, pre-trigger buffering, clip duration tracking. Marginal case — would be cleaner with small helper for pre-trigger ring, but not urgent.

title: Concurrent appends can corrupt the JSONL log
reason: Real pattern but accepted as consistent with existing feedback.rs writer; records <4KB so POSIX O_APPEND is atomic in practice. Filed for revisit under multi-user scenarios.

title: Function `detect_invoked_from` has cyclomatic complexity 10
reason: Priority-ordered env-var checks with early returns ARE the design; refactoring to a table or loop adds indirection without reducing effective branching.

title: Parent directory creation failures are silently ignored
reason: Fixed by propagating create_dir_all error via with_context.

title: Test for missing git repo does not verify behavior
reason: Fixed by testing against filesystem root instead of a tempdir that could inherit outer .git.

title: Use of `unsafe` block
reason: Rust 2024 edition requires unsafe around std::env::set_var and remove_var; this is the correct idiom.

title: Use of `.unwrap()` may panic at runtime
reason: Standard test idiom; panic IS the desired behavior for test setup failures.

title: Caller override test does not verify the override is persisted
reason: The test DOES assert rec[invoked_from] == my-ci-job on the line after the review completes.

title: Deep review tools are rooted at the process working directory instead of the fil
reason: Fixed: introduced deep_tool_root(file_path) helper that delegates to pipeline::find_project_root; applied to both sequential and parallel review paths.

title: Feedback verdict is accepted as an arbitrary string
reason: Fixed: added clap PossibleValuesParser restricting to tp/fp/partial/wontfix at parse time; 2 RED/GREEN tests added.

title: The `since` date parameter is free-form and not validated
reason: Fixed: added parse_since_date validator using chrono::NaiveDate::parse_from_str to reject malformed dates at parse time; 2 RED/GREEN tests added.

title: Suppression-rate zero check truncates
reason: Real bug: sum of u32 values cast back to u32 wrapped at 2^32 to 0, returning 0.0 instead of the correct ratio. Fixed by computing denom in u64.

title: Missing repo values are merged with a real repo named
reason: Real collision: any repo literally named 'unknown' would be indistinguishable from None. Fixed by using '(no repo)' sentinel (parens are disallowed in git repo names).

title: accepts `0`, which can produce invalid rolling-window behavior
reason: Fixed: added parse_rolling_n validator that rejects 0 at parse time; RED/GREEN test added.

title: Seed step does not assert that
reason: Fixed: every seed invocation now asserts code(0); silent seed failure would have masked real test regressions.

title: test does not verify repo grouping behavior
reason: Fixed: now asserts a 'quorum' slice exists with exactly 5 reviews, proving grouping semantics work (fixture file is inside the quorum repo).

title: Use of `.unwrap()` may panic at runtime
reason: serde_json::to_string_pretty on a locally-constructed serde_json::Value built from the json! macro cannot fail in practice — no cyclic refs, no custom Serialize impls, all values are strings/numbers/bools/arrays/objects.

title: Use of `.unwrap()` may panic at runtime
reason: Standard test idiom; panic IS the desired behavior for test setup failures.

title: Sparkline scale is corrupted by NaN/
reason: Fixed: filter non-finite values before min/max fold so infinity no longer collapses finite points to level 0. Added 2 RED/GREEN tests asserting finite points produce >1 distinct glyphs even with NaN/inf present.

title: trend_arrow reports invalid numeric input as flat
reason: Fixed: added is_finite() guard on both endpoints; non-finite now explicitly returns flat (documented as 'unknown') instead of silently relying on NaN comparison semantics. 2 RED/GREEN tests added.

title: truncate_key can panic or behave incorrectly for zero-width input
reason: Fixed: handle width==0 and width==1 explicitly; width-1 underflow on 0 eliminated. 2 RED/GREEN tests added.

title: Seed data depends on executing the real
reason: Fixed: added env_remove(QUORUM_API_KEY) to the quorum() test helper so seed runs don't depend on developer shell env.

title: By-repo test hardcodes the repository key to
reason: Fixed: added current_repo_basename() helper that derives the expected key from the test's CWD .git root at runtime; forked checkouts with different names now work.

title: By-caller JSON test does not verify the review counts per caller
reason: Fixed: test now asserts script-a has exactly 3 reviews and script-b has exactly 2 (matching seed data).

title: Unparenthesized OR clauses change query semantics when combined with other terms
reason: Fixed per quorum's suggestion: emit `(primary_text:X OR primary_text:Y)` wrapped in parens. Verified via TDD — test_builder_emits_grouped_expanded_clause asserts the grouped form. Even though the deeper bug was in temporal_parser (dateparser hijacking numeric 'or' patterns), quorum's concern about precedence-fragility was independently correct and worth hardening the BM25 emitter against future AND/OR mixing.

title: Phrase detection misses phrases adjacent to punctuation
reason: Real bug. Pre-existing code, not introduced by this PR — space-padded phrase matching in _build_paradedb_query indeed misses phrases followed/preceded by punctuation (e.g. 'MCAS,' would not trigger the 'mast cell activation syndrome' boost). Calibrator's precedent (sim=1.00 tokenization bugs) is accurate. Not fixed in this PR to keep disjunction scope tight; will file as a follow-up and fix with TDD in its own PR. Regex-boundary-based detection (quorum's suggested fix) is the right approach.

title: API key validation mishandles repeated headers
reason: Real bug (Node headers can be string[]); confirmed by both third-opinion and PAL.

title: Basic auth parsing breaks when the password contains a colon
reason: Confirmed by TP and PAL; fix is indexOf(':') + slice.

title: Authorization scheme matching is incorrectly case-sensitive
reason: Confirmed by PAL; RFC 7235 spec issue; TP didn't surface it (gap).

title: Database connection is not protected by a finally block or context manager
reason: Confirmed by TP (MED) and PAL (HIGH). Severity more aligned with medium than critical, but the bug is real.

title: Placeholder diagnosis text is inserted as real medical memory
reason: Confirmed by TP and PAL. Quorum severity Critical is appropriate — placeholder text poisons medical data store.

title: Sync is not idempotent and will duplicate memories on every run
reason: Confirmed by PAL (MED). Quorum was one of only two tools to catch this.

title: Configured `fp_suppress_count` is ignored; suppression threshold is hardcoded
reason: Real dead code. Removed the field from CalibratorConfig, Default impl, and one test literal. Logic uses hardcoded weight thresholds (1.5 full, 1.0/0.5 soft) — the actual tunables.

title: Duplicate feedback precedents are counted multiple times and can over-suppress or over-boost findings
reason: Intended design. Each FeedbackEntry is an independent verdict. Three thumbs-up on the same pattern SHOULD weigh more than one — that's the vote tallying signal, not a bug.

title: Comments still describe Wontfix as contributing to suppression after the code made it inert
reason: Real stale comment left in my prior commit. Updated comments at lines 137, 195, 349, 405 to reflect wontfix is inert (retained only for trace diagnostics).

title: Rule misses lowercase and mixed-case header names despite HTTP being case-insensitive
reason: Correct — HTTP header names are case-insensitive. Rewrote rule to use regex constraint (?i)['\"]access-control-allow-origin['\"] so any case variant matches. Added 3 case-variant tests.

title: Detection is limited to setHeader and header missing common response APIs
reason: Valid — expanded METHOD constraint to include 'set' (covers Next.js/Fetch Response.headers.set style). Added fixture test.

title: Cyclomatic complexity of auto_correct_mg_g_confusion is 15
reason: Valid finding. Refactored pattern checks to use independent corrected_this flag instead of elif chain, improving readability while keeping CC manageable for a method that inherently needs multiple correction paths.

title: Falsey checks on pct/mg skip zero values — use isinstance instead
reason: Critical bug. 0.0 is a valid measurement (e.g., ND cannabinoids stored as 0.0). Changed to isinstance(pct, (int, float)) checks throughout auto_correct_mg_g_confusion.

title: Comment claims plausibility guard but code doesn't implement it
reason: Pattern 2 comment said it checks plausibility but just blindly divided by 10. Added pct_limit guard using MINOR_CANN_MAX_FLOWER dict to ensure the correction only fires when the corrected value would be plausible.

title: Pattern 3 blocked by elif chain — never fires after Pattern 1/2
reason: Structural bug. Pattern 3 (minor cannabinoid ceiling) was in an elif after Pattern 1 and 2, so it could never trigger for compounds that didn't match those patterns. Restructured to independent if blocks with corrected_this flag.

title: import copy is local instead of module-level
reason: Minor style issue but valid per PEP 8. Moved import copy to module level with other imports.

title: Rule misses common object-based and header-map forms that also set Access-Control-Allow-Origin
reason: Real coverage gap for object-literal forms like res.set({'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*'}). Deferring to a follow-up issue — ast-grep object-key matching requires more complex rule syntax.

title: Pattern is too broad and can flag unrelated .set() calls outside HTTP response context
reason: The HEADER constraint regex (?i)['\"]access-control-allow-origin['\"] limits this very strongly — theoretical FP requires someone to call myMap.set('Access-Control-Allow-Origin', '*') on a non-response object, which does not appear in real code. Accepted breadth cost for catching Fetch Response.headers.set.
